description:
Fix the layout of the edit view in the spec editor on a card

(Duplicated from KB-083)

steps.name: Add CSS for Spec Tab Layout

log.outcome: The specification is well-structured and addresses a focused UI layout issue. The mission is clear, steps have concrete outcomes, and the file scope is accurate. Testing requirements are comprehensive with both automated and manual verification steps. The CSS approach described will resolve the flex container nesting issue preventing the textarea from filling available height.

plan review requested for Step 1 (Add CSS for Spec Tab Layout)

log.outcome: The plan correctly identifies the root cause: the `.detail-section` wrapper in TaskDetailModal.tsx lacks flex constraints, preventing the SpecEditor from filling available vertical space. The proposed `.detail-section--spec` class with `display: flex; flex-direction: column; flex: 1; min-height: 0` is the correct CSS solution for flexbox filling behavior.

log.outcome: The plan for Step 2 is straightforward and will achieve its stated outcomes. The worker needs to add the `detail-section--spec` class to the spec tab wrapper in TaskDetailModal.tsx. I note that the CSS for `.detail-section--spec` already exists in styles.css (lines 1325-1341), so Step 1 appears to already be complete in the current codebase.
